feedback.text: Game publishers are stealing the games we have bought and paid for! As a major player of video games I have, through many years, seen a troubling development. A need to be constantly online to be able to access games, in spite of it not being necessary for playing said games. I do see the point of anti piracy protection. But when game publishers choose to end support for games we have legally bought and played for years, we are left with games that we are unable to play. If publishers shut down the anti piracy servers or multiplayer servers they should be obliged to release a patch that lets us play our own games. They are the ones removing access to games we own and are thus in fact stealing the game from us. They are leaving the game without support and access, but we should be able to take the risk and support it ourselves and still be able to access the game. We need to have laws that ensure game publishers leave their games in a playable state when they, of their own volition, choose to end a game. With regards to online games they should also let us set up our own servers to play the game we own. We have paid to be able to play a game without an end date, that they only put out later, and thus when they choose to end their own servers they should release server code to let us play our own games. The law needs to catch up to our digital world and not let game publishers steal our games from under us, when they choose to shut down their access points. It is not our fault that they deem the games not worth supporting when we as players still enjoy the games.
